title: Code Structure
description: Understand the basic skeleton of every C++ program.

slides.title: Anatomy of C++ 🦴
slides.content:
Let's break down the boilerplate code from our Hello World program.

Every C++ program has a specific structure it must follow.

slides.title: The Skeleton
slides.content: Take another look at this code:

slides.title: #include <iostream> 📚
slides.content:
The line `#include <iostream>` is a **header file library**.

It lets us work with input and output objects, like `std::cout`. Think of it as importing a tool kit you need to display text to the screen.

quizData.question: What is the purpose of #include <iostream>?

options.text: It includes tools for input and output operations.

quizData.explanation: iostream stands for input-output stream. It provides the tools like `std::cout` to print to the screen.

slides.title: int main() 🏁
slides.content:
`int main()` is the **main function**.

This is the starting point of every C++ program! Any code inside its curly brackets `{}` will be executed when the program runs.

slides.title: return 0; 🛑
slides.content: The statement `return 0;` at the end of the `main()` function tells the operating system that the program has successfully finished running without any errors.

quizData.question: Where does a C++ program start executing its code?
quizData.explanation: The main() function is the entry point for all C++ programs.

slides.title: using namespace std;
